metadata.text: This policy establishes First Community Bank's (the "Bank") compliance program under the Bank Secrecy Act (BSA), 31 U.S.C. §§ 5311-5336, and implementing regulations at 31 C.F.R. Chapter X, and the USA PATRIOT Act. The Bank is committed to detecting and reporting financial crimes, including money laundering, terrorist financing, and other illicit activities.
This policy applies to all Bank employees, officers, directors, and contractors. All products, services, and customer relationships of the Bank are subject to this policy.

The Bank's BSA/AML Program is designed to comply with:
- Bank Secrecy Act (31 U.S.C. §§ 5311-5336)
- USA PATRIOT Act (31 U.S.C. § 5318(h))
- FinCEN regulations (31 C.F.R. Chapter X)
- Federal Reserve Regulation H and Y
- FFIEC BSA/AML Examination Manual

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

metadata.section_title: Four Pillars of the BSA/AML Program
metadata.text:
The Bank maintains a BSA/AML Program based on four pillars:
(a) Policies, procedures, and internal controls
(b) A designated BSA Compliance Officer
(c) Ongoing training for appropriate personnel
(d) Independent testing (audit)

The Bank shall designate a qualified BSA Compliance Officer responsible for:
- Day-to-day oversight of the BSA/AML Program
- Filing required reports with FinCEN
- Ensuring adequate training for all applicable staff
- Reporting to the Board of Directors at least annually

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 3: CUSTOMER DUE DILIGENCE (CDD) AND KNOW YOUR CUSTOMER (KYC)
metadata.text:
At account opening, the Bank shall collect and verify:
- Legal name
- Date of birth (for individuals)
- Address
- Identification number (SSN, EIN, or foreign equivalent)

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 3: CUSTOMER DUE DILIGENCE (CDD) AND KNOW YOUR CUSTOMER (KYC)
metadata.text:
The Bank shall assess the risk level of each customer based on:
- Customer type (individual, business, non-profit)
- Geographic location
- Products and services used
- Expected account activity

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 3: CUSTOMER DUE DILIGENCE (CDD) AND KNOW YOUR CUSTOMER (KYC)
metadata.text:
For legal entity customers, the Bank shall collect beneficial ownership information identifying:
- Each individual who owns 25% or more of the legal entity
- One individual who controls the legal entity

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 3: CUSTOMER DUE DILIGENCE (CDD) AND KNOW YOUR CUSTOMER (KYC)
metadata.text:
Higher-risk customers shall be subject to Enhanced Due Diligence including:
- Additional documentation of source of funds
- More frequent review of account activity
- Senior management approval for account opening
- Ongoing monitoring at least annually

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The Bank utilizes an automated transaction monitoring system configured to identify:
- Unusual cash transactions
- Structuring activity
- Rapid movement of funds
- Transactions inconsistent with customer profile

The Bank shall file a Currency Transaction Report (FinCEN Form 104) for:
- Cash transactions exceeding $10,000 in a single business day
- Multiple transactions by or on behalf of the same person exceeding $10,000
The Bank shall not assist any customer in structuring transactions to evade CTR reporting requirements. Detected structuring shall be reported via Suspicious Activity Report.

The Bank shall file a SAR with FinCEN when it knows, suspects, or has reason to suspect that a transaction:
- Involves funds from illegal activity
- Is designed to evade BSA requirements
- Lacks lawful purpose
- Involves a violation of law or regulation
Threshold: $5,000 for known or suspected violations; $2,000 for certain categories

The existence of a SAR filing shall not be disclosed to the subject of the report or any other party, except as permitted by law.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The following customer types require heightened scrutiny:
- Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs) and their family members
- Foreign financial institutions
- Money services businesses (MSBs)
- Cash-intensive businesses
- Non-profit organizations operating internationally
- Customers in high-risk jurisdictions
The Bank shall perform enhanced due diligence on foreign correspondent banking relationships including assessment of foreign institution's AML program.

Private banking accounts shall be subject to Enhanced Due Diligence and senior management oversight.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
The Bank shall screen all customers, transactions, and counterparties against OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list and applicable sanctions lists.

Transactions involving sanctioned parties or countries shall be blocked and reported to OFAC as required.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

All Bank employees shall complete BSA/AML training:
- New employees: within 30 days of hire
- All employees: annually thereafter
- BSA-specific staff: more frequent specialized training

Training shall cover:
- Recognition of suspicious activity
- Reporting obligations
- Consequences of non-compliance
- Red flags specific to employee's role

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The Bank shall conduct an independent audit of the BSA/AML Program at least annually. The audit shall assess:
- Adequacy of internal controls
- Accuracy of CTR and SAR filings
- Effectiveness of training
- Quality of transaction monitoring

The audit shall be conducted by qualified internal audit staff or an external firm with BSA expertise. Results shall be reported to the Board of Directors and Audit Committee.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The Bank shall maintain BSA-related records for five years from the date of the transaction or account closing, including:
- CIP records
- CTR filings
- SAR filings (maintained confidentially)
- Beneficial ownership certifications
- Wire transfer records

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Employees shall report suspected policy violations to the BSA Compliance Officer or through the Bank's ethics hotline.

Violations of this policy may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination, as well as potential civil and criminal penalties.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

POLICY APPROVAL

Approved: Chief Compliance Officer
Date: January 1, 2026
Board Acknowledgment: January 15, 2026

This policy supersedes all prior BSA/AML policies and procedures.
Next scheduled review: January 1, 2027

This policy establishes First Community Bank's commitment to fair, equitable, and non-discriminatory lending practices in compliance with the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA), Regulation B, the Fair Housing Act (FHA), and the Home Mortgage Disclosure Act (HMDA).

This policy applies to all credit products offered by the Bank, including:
- Residential mortgage loans
- Home equity loans and lines of credit
- Consumer installment loans
- Small business loans
- Agricultural loans
- Commercial real estate loans

- Equal Credit Opportunity Act (15 U.S.C. § 1691 et seq.)
- Regulation B (12 C.F.R. Part 1002) — CFPB
- Fair Housing Act (42 U.S.C. §§ 3601-3619)
- Home Mortgage Disclosure Act (12 U.S.C. § 2801 et seq.)
- Regulation C (12 C.F.R. Part 1003) — CFPB
- Community Reinvestment Act (12 U.S.C. § 2901 et seq.)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The Bank shall not discriminate against any credit applicant on the basis of:
- Race or color
- Religion
- National origin
- Sex (including gender identity and sexual orientation)
- Marital status
- Age (provided the applicant has legal capacity to contract)
- The applicant's receipt of income from public assistance programs
- The applicant's exercise of rights under the Consumer Credit Protection Act
- Familial status (FHA only — applicable to residential mortgage lending)
- Disability (FHA only)
The Bank shall not treat applicants differently based on any prohibited basis, whether intentionally or through facially neutral policies that have a disparate impact.

The Bank shall not discourage applicants from applying for credit on any prohibited basis, including through:
- Steering applicants to less favorable products
- Providing less information or assistance
- Expressing a preference or limitation based on prohibited characteristics

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
The Bank shall apply the same underwriting criteria to all similarly situated applicants regardless of any prohibited basis.

The following factors are permitted in credit decisions:
- Credit history and credit score
- Income, employment, and assets
- Debt-to-income ratio
- Collateral value and loan-to-value ratio
- Purpose of the loan

The Bank may offer Special Purpose Credit Programs to meet special social needs, provided such programs comply with Regulation B § 1002.8, including:
- Written program plan
- Specific class of persons to be benefited
- Terms no more favorable than necessary
Board approval required before implementation of any SPCP.

Loan pricing shall be based on objective, risk-based criteria documented in writing. Loan officers may deviate from standard pricing only within established exception procedures, subject to fair lending review.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The Bank shall provide written adverse action notices as required by ECOA and Regulation B within required timeframes:
- Completed application: within 30 days
- Incomplete application: within 30 days of receiving an incomplete application
- Counteroffer not accepted: within 90 days

Adverse action notices shall include:
- Statement that credit was denied
- Name and address of creditor
- Principal reasons for adverse action (minimum 4, maximum specified by regulation)
- ECOA notice (right to know reasons)
- Credit score information (if applicable)
- Credit reporting agency information (if applicable)

The Bank shall not use medical information in credit decisions except as specifically permitted under Regulation B.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 5: HOME MORTGAGE DISCLOSURE ACT (HMDA) COMPLIANCE
metadata.text:
For covered mortgage loan applications, the Bank shall collect and record required HMDA data points including:
- Applicant demographic information (ethnicity, race, sex, age, income)
- Loan characteristics (amount, type, purpose, lien status)
- Property information (location, type, value)
- Action taken and date

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 5: HOME MORTGAGE DISCLOSURE ACT (HMDA) COMPLIANCE
metadata.text:
The Bank shall maintain data quality controls to ensure HMDA data accuracy, including:
- Automated data validation at point of entry
- Annual data scrub process before LAR submission
- Testing for data completeness and consistency

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 5: HOME MORTGAGE DISCLOSURE ACT (HMDA) COMPLIANCE
metadata.text:
The Bank shall submit its annual Loan Application Register (LAR) to the CFPB by March 1 each year for the prior calendar year.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

[PLACEHOLDER — Section 1071 implementation rules pending final effective dates]
The Bank shall monitor CFPB rulemaking under Dodd-Frank Section 1071 regarding small business lending data collection and update this policy section upon final rule implementation.

Section 1071 will require collection of demographic and credit information for covered small business credit applications, similar to HMDA for mortgage lending. The Bank's compliance date will be determined by its annual small business loan originations.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 7: FAIR LENDING MONITORING AND TESTING
metadata.text:
The Bank shall conduct an annual fair lending analysis including:
- Statistical analysis of approval and denial rates by prohibited basis
- Pricing analysis comparing similarly situated borrowers
- Geographic analysis of lending patterns (CRA Assessment Area)
- HMDA peer comparison

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 7: FAIR LENDING MONITORING AND TESTING
metadata.text: At least annually, the Bank shall conduct a comparative file review of credit decisions to identify potential disparate treatment.

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 7: FAIR LENDING MONITORING AND TESTING
metadata.text:
The Bank may conduct periodic mystery shopping to test for potential discouragement of applicants.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

All consumer-facing employees, loan officers, and credit personnel shall receive fair lending training:
- At hire: within 60 days
- Annual refresher: each calendar year

- Prohibited bases for discrimination
- Recognizing and avoiding disparate treatment
- HMDA data collection accuracy
- Adverse action notice requirements
- Special purpose credit programs

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

POLICY APPROVAL

Approved: Chief Compliance Officer / Chief Credit Officer
Date: January 1, 2026
Board Acknowledgment: January 15, 2026

This policy supersedes all prior fair lending policies.
Next scheduled review: January 1, 2027

This policy establishes First Community Bank's procedures for complying with the TILA-RESPA Integrated Disclosure (TRID) rule, which implements requirements under the Truth in Lending Act (TILA), the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA), and Regulation Z (12 C.F.R. Part 1026) and Regulation X (12 C.F.R. Part 1024).

This policy applies to all closed-end consumer credit transactions secured by real property, including:
- Purchase money mortgages
- Refinance loans
- Construction loans (if applicable)
- Home equity loans (not HELOCs — excluded from TRID)

Excluded transactions:
- Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOCs) — subject to separate TILA disclosures
- Reverse mortgages — subject to separate TILA requirements
- Loans secured by vacant land (unless construction loan)
- Commercial and investment property loans

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

A Loan Estimate must be provided after receiving six pieces of information constituting an application:
(1) Consumer's name
(2) Consumer's income
(3) Consumer's Social Security Number (for credit report)
(4) Property address
(5) Estimated value of the property
(6) Loan amount

The Loan Estimate shall be:
- Delivered or placed in mail no later than three business days after receiving an application
- Received by the consumer no later than seven business days before consummation

The Loan Estimate shall include:
- General information (loan terms, projected payments, costs at closing)
- Closing cost details (Loan Costs and Other Costs)
- Calculating cash to close
- Comparisons (APR, Total Interest Percentage)
- Other considerations (appraisal, assumption, homeowner's insurance, late payments)
- Contact information

The Bank shall comply with the following tolerance standards:

Zero Tolerance Items (no increase permitted):
- Transfer taxes
- Fees paid to the creditor for required services with no choice
- Third-party fees for services not on approved provider list
- Recording fees

10% Tolerance Items:
- Recording fees (exception when aggregate tolerance applies)
- Third-party fees for required services on approved provider list chosen by consumer
- Prepaid interest, property insurance premiums, initial escrow payments
- Charges for optional third-party services

No Tolerance Items (may change):
- Prepaid interest
- Property insurance premiums
- Initial escrow payment amounts
- Homeowners association dues (paid at closing)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The Closing Disclosure shall be:
- Received by the consumer no later than three business days before consummation
- For mailing purposes, presumed received three business days after placing in mail
The Closing Disclosure mirrors the Loan Estimate format and includes final figures for all costs, loan terms, and cash to close.

A revised Closing Disclosure is required if, before consummation:
- APR becomes inaccurate (exceeds tolerance)
- Loan product changes
- Prepayment penalty is added

A revised Closing Disclosure requires an additional three business day waiting period.

Post-consummation corrections to Closing Disclosures shall be handled pursuant to Regulation Z § 1026.19(f)(2) procedures.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

A revised Loan Estimate may be issued only upon occurrence of a valid changed circumstance:
- Extraordinary event beyond the Bank's control
- Information specific to the consumer/transaction that was inaccurate or changed
- New information not previously available
- Delay in settlement (under certain conditions)
- Rate locks
- Consumer requests changes to credit terms

A revised Loan Estimate shall be delivered within three business days of receiving information supporting a changed circumstance, and no later than four business days before consummation.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 5: ESCROW DISCLOSURES AND ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT
metadata.text:
For escrow accounts, the Bank shall provide:
- Initial Escrow Account Disclosure Statement at consummation
- Annual Escrow Account Analysis and Statement

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 5: ESCROW DISCLOSURES AND ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT
metadata.text:
The Bank shall conduct an annual escrow analysis for each escrowed loan to determine:
- Whether a surplus, shortage, or deficiency exists
- The adjusted monthly escrow payment for the coming year

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 5: ESCROW DISCLOSURES AND ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT
metadata.text:
- Surplus ≥ $50: Refund within 30 days of analysis
- Shortage: May collect over 12 months if shortage ≤ 1/12 monthly payment; otherwise spread over longer period
- Deficiency: Bank may collect in two months; shortage notice procedures apply

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 6: LOAN OFFICER AND PROCESSOR PROCEDURES
metadata.text:
The Bank's loan origination system (LOS) shall be configured to:
- Automatically generate Loan Estimates upon application receipt
- Calculate and monitor tolerance thresholds
- Generate alerts when revised disclosures may be required
- Track all disclosure delivery dates and methods

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 6: LOAN OFFICER AND PROCESSOR PROCEDURES
metadata.text:
Loan officers are responsible for:
- Collecting complete application information before triggering disclosures
- Not discouraging applications based on protected characteristics
- Accurately estimating fees and costs on the Loan Estimate
- Documenting changed circumstances before issuing revised Loan Estimates

metadata.parent_section: SECTION 6: LOAN OFFICER AND PROCESSOR PROCEDURES
metadata.text:
The Bank shall designate a TRID compliance coordinator responsible for:
- Reviewing all Closing Disclosures before delivery
- Monitoring consummation dates and waiting periods
- Maintaining a disclosure log for each loan
- Resolving tolerance cure procedures

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The Bank may cure a violation of the good faith requirement by refunding to the consumer the amount by which the tolerance was exceeded, at or before consummation or within 60 calendar days after consummation.

All tolerance cures shall be documented, including:
- Original and actual fee amounts
- Cure amount
- Date of refund
- Method of refund

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The Bank shall retain all TRID-related records for three years from the date of consummation or from the date of disclosures (whichever is longer), including:
- All versions of Loan Estimates and Closing Disclosures
- Evidence of delivery (delivery confirmation, mailing receipts)
- Documentation of changed circumstances for revised Loan Estimates
- Escrow account disclosures and analyses

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

All mortgage loan officers, processors, closers, and compliance staff shall receive TRID training:
- At hire: within 30 days
- Annual refresher: each calendar year
- Regulatory updates: within 90 days of effective date

The Bank shall conduct quarterly file reviews of closed mortgage loans to test:
- Timely delivery of Loan Estimates and Closing Disclosures
- Accuracy of disclosures
- Appropriate use of revised disclosures
- Tolerance compliance

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

POLICY APPROVAL

Approved: Chief Compliance Officer / Chief Lending Officer
Date: January 1, 2026
Board Acknowledgment: January 15, 2026

This policy supersedes all prior TRID/RESPA/TILA disclosure policies.
Next scheduled review: January 1, 2027
